nibble_unpack_u4: add+and(0xf) dep chain. iters=50M, unroll=32, approx_gops=2.96
nibble_unpack_i4: add+sbfx(0,4) dep chain. iters=50M, unroll=32, approx_gops=3.16
fadd_dep_ld_aarch64: matches fadd_dep_f64=0.949ns. long double is IEEE 754 binary64 on AArch64 ABI.
dd_twosum_dep: Knuth 2-sum. 4 FP ops/step. 1.36× scalar f64. iters=20M, unroll=8
dd_twosum_fma_dep: FMA 2-sum. No dep-chain gain vs plain — linear chain masks FMA benefit.
Scalar: 1.348ns per 106-bit extended-precision value. Only 1.36× f64 overhead.
f64x2_vadd_dep: 2×f64 per NEON insn. 0.947ns/insn = 0.473ns/value. Same latency as scalar fadd.
0.473ns per f64 value via NEON — 2.10× throughput vs scalar f64 (0.992ns).
0.237ns per f32 FMA value via NEON — 4.19× throughput vs scalar f64.
f64x2_dd_twosum_dep: 2 dd-pairs per NEON step. 1.040ns/step = 0.520ns/106-bit value.
BREAKTHROUGH: 106-bit precision at 0.520ns/value — 1.91× MORE throughput than scalar f64. NEON parallelises 2 dd-pairs; OOO overlaps 4-op chain to ~3.3 cycles/step vs expected 4.
f64x2_dd_throughput: 4 indep float64x2_t accumulators 32 steps/iter. 3.73× over dep chain (1.043ns). 0.280/2=0.140ns/106-bit value.
PEAK: 0.140ns per 106-bit DD value — 7.1× throughput vs scalar f64 (0.994ns). 3.73× speedup over dep-chain Level 5 (0.521ns).
veltkamp_split_dep: Veltkamp C=2^27+1 product split. fmul+fsub+fsub+fadd dep chain. 4.102ns/step ≈ 13cy (fmul≈4cy + 3×fadd/fsub@3cy). 3.06× Knuth 2-sum.
probe_dd_lat: FP32 float2(hi,lo) dep chain 8 steps/iter × 100k GPU iters. 3.968 ns/step per DD pair. Calibrated batch (overhead-corrected); replaces earlier 4.990 raw value.
probe_dd_tput: 4 independent FP32 float2 pairs 32 steps/iter × 100k GPU iters. 1.058 ns/step. Calibrated batch (overhead-corrected); replaces earlier 1.080 raw value.
4 independent accumulators give 3.749× speedup — close to 4× theoretical. Calibrated batch; earlier batch showed 4.64× (measurement noise above theoretical).
probe_ffma_lat: FP32 FMA dep chain 32 ops/iter × 100k GPU iters. 2.960 ns/step. Calibrated batch with host-harness iters fix.
probe_ffma_tput: FP32 FMA throughput 4 independent chains 32 ops/iter × 100k GPU iters. 2.853 ns/step. Calibrated batch.
FMA pipeline saturation: 1.038× speedup from 4 independent chains (32 ops/iter saturates AGX FP pipeline at ~1 FMA/cycle). Compare DD (8 ops/iter) which achieves 3.749× at 25% utilization.
neon_dd_fma_throughput: 4 indep float64x2_t accumulators vfmaq error term 32 steps/iter. 0.281 ns/step. FMA neutral: plain=0.279 ns (0.7% diff). Confirms FMA adds no benefit in DD throughput mode.
neon_dd_fma_throughput / f64x2_dd_throughput = 0.281/0.279 = 1.007 (FMA 0.7% slower — within noise). FMA neutrality confirmed in throughput mode.

f32_dekker_dep ISA: 11 VLIW5 bundles for 8 steps = 1.375 cycles/step. VLIW5 packs parallel Dekker sub-ops (e1||e2 in same bundle). Only 37.5% overhead vs scalar FP32 add dep chain (8-9 bundles/8 steps = 1.0-1.125 cyc/step). Wall-clock timing unreliable (GPU clock varies 2-3x between dispatches). ISA dwords: fp32=68, dekker=122 (no DCE).
Dekker 1.375cy/step vs scalar ADD 1.0cy/step = 1.375x overhead. Contrast: scalar hardware would have 4x overhead (4 dependent ADDs). VLIW5 ILP converts 4 dependent ops into 1.375 effective cycles.

probe_int32_xorshift_lat: 8 xorshift32 rounds (acc^=acc<<13;acc^=acc>>17;acc^=acc<<5) × 24 ops/iter × 400 iters. Non-foldable dep chain (verified via llvm-dis: loop body intact, shl/xor i32). Baseline for int32 native ALU.

probe_int64_xorshift_lat: 8 xorshift64 rounds × 24 ops/iter × 400 iters. AIR uses genuine shl/xor i64 (llvm-dis confirms). Dispatch = 42,170,115 ns = 2.512× int32 baseline. AGX backend lowers i64 shift/XOR to i32 pairs — no native 64-bit shift/XOR ALU.

INT64 xorshift / INT32 xorshift = 42170115 / 16803740 = 2.512×. Consistent with i64→i32 pair lowering for shift ops (shl i64 val, k requires cross-word carry: ~2.5 effective critical-path ops vs 1 for i32).

probe_int32_mul_lat: 32 dependent muls/iter — LLVM-folded to 1 mul/iter (1664525^32 mod 2^32 = 666245249). Measures single-dependent-mul latency per iter. 3,275,605 ns/dispatch (400 iters). Note: ops_per_iter claimed=32 but effective=1 after fold.

probe_int64_mul_lat: 32 dependent muls/iter — LLVM-folded to 1 mul/iter (1664525^32 mod 2^64 = -4842297668175259519). Measures single-dependent-mul latency. 4,381,112 ns/dispatch = 1.337× int32 mul. Smaller ratio than xorshift (2.512×) suggests AGX has efficient 64-bit multiply path (possibly widened 32×32→64 multiplier).

INT64 mul (folded) / INT32 mul (folded) = 4381112 / 3275605 = 1.337×. Contrasts with 2.512× for shift/XOR. AGX may have widened multiply unit serving 64-bit ops more efficiently than shift/XOR lowering.
int16/uint16 Metal AIR: all arithmetic uses i32 ops; final 'and i32 %n, 65535' truncates to 16-bit range. No native 16-bit integer ALU in AGX Metal path.
pytorch_mps int16 gemm 256×256. MPS 2.44× faster than CPU at this size. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int16 gemm 1024×1024. MPS 8.50× faster than CPU. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int32 gemm 256×256. MPS 4.05× faster than CPU. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int32 gemm 1024×1024. MPS 11.8× faster than CPU. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int64 gemm 256×256. MPS 9.77× faster than CPU — MPS parallelism overcomes int64 backend-lowering penalty. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int64 gemm 1024×1024. MPS 19.8× faster than CPU. int64/int32 MPS GEMM ratio = 2.17× (consistent with AGX i64 backend-lowering penalty). Measured 2026-04-02.
MPS int64 gemm_1024 / MPS int32 gemm_1024 = 28.27/13.02 = 2.17×. Consistent with AGX i64→i32 pair lowering observed in xorshift dep-chain (2.51×). GEMM parallelism partially masks latency cost.
pytorch_cpu uint16 GEMM: NotImplementedError — addmm_impl_cpu_ not implemented for UInt16. No unsigned integer GEMM kernel in PyTorch.
pytorch_cpu uint32 GEMM: NotImplementedError — addmm_impl_cpu_ not implemented for UInt32.
pytorch_cpu uint64 GEMM: NotImplementedError — addmm_impl_cpu_ not implemented for UInt64.
pytorch_mps uint16 GEMM: RuntimeError — Failed to create function state object for: matmul_ushort.
pytorch_mps uint32 GEMM: RuntimeError — Failed to create function state object for: matmul_uint.
pytorch_mps uint64 GEMM: RuntimeError — Failed to create function state object for: matmul_ulong.
pytorch_cpu int64 gemm 2048×2048. CPU int64/int32 ratio = 3.85× (grows vs 3.66× at 1024 — different BLAS tiling path). Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int16 gemm 2048×2048. MPS 9.13× faster than CPU. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int32 gemm 2048×2048. MPS 13.5× faster than CPU. Measured 2026-04-02.
pytorch_mps int64 gemm 2048×2048. MPS 24.1× faster than CPU. MPS int64/int32 = 2.16× (stable vs 2.17× at 1024; structural AGX i64 backend-lowering penalty confirmed). Measured 2026-04-02.
MPS int64/int32 gemm_2048 = 216.1/100.0 = 2.16× (vs 2.17× at 1024). Stable ratio confirms AGX backend-lowering penalty is structural, not an artefact of matrix size.
INT32 xorshift GPU-internal timing (commandbuffer GPUStartTime/GPUEndTime; dispatch-boundary sampling unsupported on AGXG13G). 400 dispatches × 1024 threads × 400 iters × 24 ops.
INT64 xorshift GPU-internal timing. GPU ratio = 41696077/16393367 = 2.544× (vs 2.512× wall-clock). Overhead-free GPU ratio confirms AGX i64 backend-lowering penalty is not a host-dispatch artefact.

GPU-internal INT64/INT32 xorshift ratio = 2.544×. Slightly higher than wall-clock ratio (2.512×) as expected (dispatch overhead skews wall-clock toward equality). Pure GPU execution ratio confirms structural i64→i32 pair lowering.
Folded Knuth 2-sum: LLVM reassoc folds e=delta-(s-hi) to 0 under Metal fast-math. Baseline for wide-precision GPU timing. 20 dispatches × shader_inner_iters=100000.
Folded half-precision Knuth 2-sum. gpu_ns/dispatch ≈ baseline (3.47ms vs 3.43ms) — CONFIRMED FOLDED by Metal reassoc fast-math. Measured 2026-04-02.
Folded QH4 4-level cascade. gpu_ns/dispatch ≈ baseline (3.28ms) — CONFIRMED FOLDED. All 4-level error terms folded to 0 by Metal reassoc. Measured 2026-04-02.
Genuine Knuth 2-sum: #pragma clang fp reassociate(off) preserves error term. 1.70× baseline. Extra compute: 2.40ms/100K iters = 24ns/inner_iter (11-cycle dep chain depth). Measured 2026-04-02.
Genuine float32 DD / folded DD = 5.83/3.43 = 1.70×. GPU-internal ratio. Measured 2026-04-02.
Genuine half-precision Knuth 2-sum. 1.82× baseline. Extra compute: 2.80ms/100K — 1.17× MORE expensive than float32 genuine (2.40ms). AGX likely promotes half to float internally. Measured 2026-04-02.
half genuine / float32 genuine extra compute = 2.80ms / 2.40ms = 1.17×. AGX half has NO latency advantage over float32 for serial dep chains — use float32 as base for wide precision.
Genuine QH4 4-level cascade. 4.99× baseline. Extra compute: 13.67ms/100K — 4.88× more than fp16x2_genuine. Cascade is strictly serial across carry levels; AGX has no OOO overlap. Measured 2026-04-02.
QH4 genuine / QH2 genuine extra compute = 13.67/2.80 = 4.88×. Consistent with QH4 dep chain being ~4.88× deeper than QH2 (3 serial error levels vs 1).

Veltkamp DS-multiply (r600 Q.q pattern). 1.40× baseline. Extra compute: 1.39ms/100K = 14ns/inner_iter. LLVM parallelizes H-path (step^1..step^8 constants); L-path fmul+fadd dep chain is genuine serial. Measured 2026-04-02.

Software FP8_e4m3 round-trip dep chain. 86× baseline. Extra compute: 291.9ms/100K = 2919ns/inner_iter. FP8 decode+add+encode ≈ 116 ops per 8-step iter. Not viable without native FP8. Measured 2026-04-02.
Software FP8x8 / folded DD GPU ratio = 295.3ms / 3.43ms = 86×. Dominated by encode/decode overhead.
8 serial fadd dep chain (runtime delta, reassociate-off). Measured 2026-04-02. extra=(4510334-3153956)=1356378 ns for 700K fadd steps → 1.695 ns/fadd.
8 serial fmul dep chain (runtime step, reassociate-off). Measured 2026-04-02. extra=(4231204-3153956)=1077248 ns for 700K fmul steps → 1.347 ns/fmul.
AGX FP32 fadd dep-chain latency = 1.695 ns/step (X_fadd = 1,186,831/7/100K). ~2.17 effective cycles at 1.278 GHz. Derived from 8-fadd vs folded-1-fadd timing difference.
AGX FP32 fmul dep-chain latency = 1.347 ns/step (X_fmul = 1,077,248/8/100K). ~1.72 effective cycles at 1.278 GHz. Derived from 8-fmul vs folded-1-fadd timing.
AGX fmul dep-chain latency / fadd dep-chain latency = 1.695/1.347 = 1.259× (fadd is slower). Explains DS-mul genuine being 1.64× cheaper than DD genuine.
Per-dispatch GPU overhead (commandbuffer GPUStartTime/GPUEndTime). C = 3,323,503 - 169,547 = 3,153,956 ns. Includes GPU scheduling, encoder setup, thread dispatch overhead.

Genuine DS-multiply with H AND L both serial (reassociate-off). H-parallel optimization (LLVM precomputing step^n) is a no-op: ds_mul_lat=4,807,808 ≈ ds_mul_genuine=4,811,554 (L-chain bottleneck in both). Measured 2026-04-02.

DS-mul genuine extra compute / DD genuine extra compute = 1,488,051/2,443,872 = 0.609×. DS-multiply is 1.64× cheaper per dep-chain step because its L-path (fmul+fma+fadd) benefits from lower fmul latency vs DD's all-fadd path.
AGX FP32 fma(a,b,c) dep-chain probe: 8 serial fma per iter x 100K shader iters. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,748,167 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP32 fma dep-chain latency = (4,748,167 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 1.993 ns/fma. FMA is SLOWER than fadd (1.695 ns) and fmul (1.347 ns) for serial dep chains. Counterintuitive — AGX fma pipeline is deeper.

AGX fma dep-chain latency / fadd dep-chain latency = 1.993/1.695 = 1.176×. FMA 18% slower than fadd in serial chains. Hypothesis: AGX fma pipeline supports correct rounding, longer pipeline depth than fadd.
AGX fma dep-chain latency / fmul dep-chain latency = 1.993/1.347 = 1.480×. FMA 48% slower than fmul in serial chains. Confirms fmul < fadd < fma latency ordering on AGX.

DS-mul genuine step latency = (4,811,554 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 2.072 ns/step ≈ 1 FMA + 4% overhead. AGX overlaps fmul(H) and fadd(L) within the FMA pipeline depth window — FMA is the sole rate-limiting op on the L dep chain.
AGX INT32 imul dep-chain probe: 8 serial (acc=acc*step+offset) per iter x 100K shader iters. gpu_ns/dispatch=5,854,967 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX INT32 imul dep-chain latency = (5,854,967 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 3.376 ns/op. Integer multiply runs on a SEPARATE pipeline from FP. ~2.0× slower than FP32 fadd (1.695 ns). Implies dedicated int-mul unit with ~2-cycle effective latency.
AGX INT32 imul / FP32 fadd dep-chain latency ratio = 3.376/1.695 = 1.992×. Integer multiply pipeline is ~2× deeper than FP32 add on AGX.
AGX FP32 fadd throughput probe: 8 INDEPENDENT fadd accumulators (acc0..acc7 += delta) per iter. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,026,300 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP32 fadd throughput (8 independent ops) = (4,026,300 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 1.090 ns per 8-op issue slot. Latency/throughput ratio: 1.695/1.090 = 1.55×. AGX can sustain ~1.55× faster fadd throughput than serial dep-chain allows.
AGX FP32 fmul throughput probe: 8 INDEPENDENT fmul accumulators. gpu_ns/dispatch=3,755,167 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP32 fmul throughput (8 independent ops) = (3,755,167 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 0.751 ns per 8-op issue slot. Latency/throughput ratio: 1.347/0.751 = 1.79×. fmul throughput faster than fadd throughput (0.751 vs 1.090 ns).
AGX FP32 fma throughput probe: 8 INDEPENDENT fma accumulators. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,565,467 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP32 fma throughput (8 independent ops) = (4,565,467 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 1.764 ns per 8-op issue slot. SURPRISING: fma throughput (1.764 ns) SLOWER than fadd throughput (1.090 ns) even with 8 independent chains — implies AGX fma unit has lower issue bandwidth than fadd/fmul units.
AGX FP16 fadd dep-chain probe: 8 serial half fadd per iter x 100K shader iters. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,834,900 (30 dispatches width=1024). Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP16 fadd dep-chain latency = (4,834,900 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 2.101 ns/op. SLOWER than FP32 fadd (1.695 ns) by 24%. Confirms AGX widens half→float internally on dep-chain paths. Same direction as fp16x2_dd_genuine finding.
AGX FP16 fmul dep-chain probe. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,768,000. Measured 2026-04-02.
AGX FP16 fmul dep-chain latency = (4,768,000 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 2.018 ns/op. SLOWER than FP32 fmul (1.347 ns) by 50%. FP16 widening penalty is LARGER for fmul than fadd on AGX.
AGX FP16 fma dep-chain probe. gpu_ns/dispatch=4,758,733. Measured 2026-04-02.

AGX FP16 fma dep-chain latency = (4,758,733 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 2.006 ns/op. All FP16 ops cluster at ~2.0 ns (range 2.006–2.101 ns) regardless of op type. Suggests a common widening overhead of ~0.3–0.7 ns dominates over the op-type latency difference.

AGX FP16 fadd/fmul/fma dep-chain latencies all cluster at ~2.0 ns (2.006–2.101 ns). The op-type spread seen in FP32 (1.347–1.993 ns range) collapses. Implies the half→float widening overhead masks the FP32 pipeline type differences.
AGX Veltkamp product split dep-chain: 8 steps per iter, each ~7 FP ops (fmul+fsub+fsub+fsub+fmul+fma+fmul+fadd on critical path). gpu_ns/dispatch=12,544,933. Measured 2026-04-02.

Veltkamp split step latency = (12,544,933 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 11.739 ns per 8-step macro-op (1.468 ns per individual FP op in the dep chain = ~fadd latency). Confirms each FP op in the Veltkamp chain costs one standard AGX ALU latency slot with no hidden stalls.

AGX threadgroup (LDS) load dep-chain probe. gpu_ns/dispatch=3,601,067. COMPILER-ELIDED: result (0.559 ns/step) is below fmul latency floor — Metal compiler hoisted threadgroup array into registers. NOT a genuine LDS latency measurement. Needs volatile or asm barrier to defeat. (2026-04-02)

AGX global memory load dep-chain: 1024 threads × address-dependent loads on shared 4KB output buffer. 58.62 ns/load. Reflects bank contention (1024 threads on 64 cache lines), NOT single-thread L1 hit latency. Context: ~75 cycles of effective serialized access time. (2026-04-02)

AGX simd_sum dep-chain latency: 8 serial simd_sum(acc) calls per shader iter. 28.31 ns/simd_sum ≈ 36 cycles for a full 32-lane cross-lane reduction on AGX. Cross-lane ops are ~17× more expensive than a single FP32 fadd (1.695 ns). (2026-04-02)
AGX simd_sum latency / FP32 fadd latency = 28.31 / 1.695 = 16.70×. Cross-lane reductions incur significant synchronization cost on AGX.
AGX threadgroup atomic_fetch_add dep-chain: serial chain on threadgroup atomic_uint (gid==0 only). gpu_ns/dispatch=74,836,533. (2026-04-02)

AGX threadgroup atomic_fetch_add serial dep-chain latency = (74,836,533 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 89.60 ns/atomic ≈ 115 cycles. Serial atomics on threadgroup memory are ~53× slower than FP32 fadd (1.695 ns).
AGX device-buffer atomic_fetch_add dep-chain (gid==0 serial chain on device atomic_uint). gpu_ns/dispatch=118,168,600. (2026-04-02)

AGX global device-buffer atomic_fetch_add serial dep-chain latency = (118,168,600 - 3,153,956) / 800,000 = 143.77 ns/atomic ≈ 184 cycles. Global atomics 60% slower than threadgroup atomics (143 vs 90 ns). Both consistent with GPU atomic serialization at cache/memory controller level.

AGX genuine LDS latency (volatile threadgroup, cross-thread access): 43.66 ns per step (load+store). Per individual LDS access ~21.8 ns ≈ 28 GPU cycles. Previous 0.56 ns was register-elided. (2026-04-03)
AGX LDS + threadgroup_barrier combined: 69.10 ns/step. Barrier alone: 69.10 - 43.66 = 25.44 ns ≈ 33 GPU cycles. (2026-04-03)
AGX threadgroup_barrier isolation: 69.10 - 43.66 = 25.44 ns ≈ 33 GPU cycles per barrier call in serial dep chain.
AGX genuine warm L1 global memory load latency (per-thread slot, no cross-thread contention): 10.07 ns ≈ 13 GPU cycles. Previous 58.6 ns was 1024-thread bank contention. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series scalar FP16 fadd dep-chain latency: 0.945 ns/op — IDENTICAL to FP32 fadd (0.945 ns). M-series FEAT_FP16 has zero latency penalty for scalar half-precision. (2026-04-03)

AArch64 M-series scalar FP16 fmul dep-chain latency: 1.306 ns/op. Slightly slower than FP16 fadd (0.945 ns); same direction as FP32 where fmul may differ. Compare: GPU fp16 fmul = 2.018 ns (54% slower). (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series INT32 mul dep-chain latency: 0.979 ns/op ≈ FP32 fadd (0.945 ns). CPU int multiply = FP add latency tier. Compare: GPU INT32 imul = 3.376 ns (3.57× slower). (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series INT64 mul dep-chain latency: 0.944 ns/op. Same tier as FP32 add. Consistent with M-series unified multiply pipeline. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series FSQRT dep-chain latency: 4.870 ns/op ≈ 5 cycles. Hardware sqrt unit. 5.15× slower than FP64 fadd. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series log() dep-chain latency: 14.497 ns/op ≈ 15 cycles. libm software implementation — no hardware log instruction on AArch64. 15.3× slower than FP64 fadd. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series exp() dep-chain latency: 14.879 ns/op ≈ 15 cycles. libm software. 15.7× slower than FP64 fadd. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series CRC32 (FEAT_CRC32) dep-chain latency: 0.998 ns/op ≈ 1 cycle. Hardware CRC32 instruction. Same tier as FP add. (2026-04-03)
AArch64 M-series CLZ dep-chain latency: 0.645 ns/op — sub-cycle effective. Count-leading-zeros is extremely fast on M-series. (2026-04-03)
CPU: 4 independent scalar DD pairs throughput: 0.530 ns for 4-pair step = 0.132 ns per DD pair in throughput mode. OOO engine overlaps 4 independent Knuth two-sum chains. (2026-04-03)

CPU: 4 independent scalar FMA-DD pairs throughput: 0.532 ns for 4-pair step = 0.133 ns per pair. FMA version essentially same as fadd version in throughput mode — FMA advantage is minimal for OOO-overlapped chains. (2026-04-03)

NEON vectorised DD throughput (f64x2_dd_throughput probe): 0.289 ns per vector = 0.144 ns per DD value. FASTEST measured: 106-bit precision at 0.144 ns/value, 6.6× faster than scalar f64 for high-throughput DD. (2026-04-03)
CPU AArch64 Veltkamp DS-multiply dep-chain: 5.440 ns/step (~7 FP ops/step = 0.777 ns/FP-op). Compare: GPU Veltkamp = 11.739 ns/step. CPU is 2.16× faster. (2026-04-03)

AGX FP32 FMA throughput with 16 independent accumulators: 10,915,400 ns/dispatch. DEGRADES vs 8-acc (1.764ns→4.851ns). AIR confirmed 16 independent @air.fma.f32 instructions. Register spills at 16 float acc + 16 step/delta vars = 32 registers. 8-acc IS the register-optimal point. (2026-04-03)

FMA throughput anomaly RESOLVED: the 1.764ns/8acc result was near the register pressure boundary (8acc×3regs=24), not a half-rate FMA unit. Philip's '1-cycle FFMA throughput' remains plausible at lower register pressure; our 8-acc fma probe was already spilling.
AGX FP32 fadd throughput with 16 independent accumulators: 8,248,833 ns/dispatch = 3.184 ns/op. Degrades from 8-acc (1.090 ns) by 2.9×. Confirms register pressure at 16 accumulators. (2026-04-03)

AGX FP16 fadd throughput (8 independent accumulators): 4,136,600 ns/dispatch = 1.228 ns/op. 12.7% SLOWER than fp32 fadd throughput (1.090 ns). Rosenzweig's 'more 16-bit ALUs' throughput advantage NOT demonstrated at 8 accumulators. (2026-04-03)

AGX FP16 fmul throughput (8 independent accumulators): 3,822,767 ns/dispatch = 0.836 ns/op. 11.3% SLOWER than fp32 fmul throughput (0.751 ns). Both fp16 tp probes slightly slower than fp32 — consistent with same-pipeline execution, fp16 advantage requires >8 independent chains. (2026-04-03)

AGX FP16 fma throughput (8 independent accumulators): 3,742,367 ns/dispatch = 0.736 ns/op = 0.94 cycles. Sub-cycle result is suspicious — short net compute time (588K ns) may be measurement noise or the fp16 fma unit IS genuinely fast. Needs confirmation. (2026-04-03)
AGX FP32 reciprocal (1/x) dep-chain latency: 10,038,300 ns/dispatch = 8.605 ns/op ≈ 11 cycles. Special reciprocal unit; ~6.4× fadd latency. (2026-04-03)

AGX FP32 reciprocal throughput (8 independent accumulators): 8,169,233 ns/dispatch = 6.269 ns/op ≈ 8 cycles. Philip says 6-cycle throughput; our 8-acc probe gives 8 cycles — need more chains to saturate the recip pipeline. (2026-04-03)
AGX FP32 rsqrt dep-chain latency: 10,677,600 ns/dispatch = 9.405 ns/op ≈ 12 cycles. Philip says 8-cycle throughput; our latency = 12 cycles. Special rsqrt unit. (2026-04-03)

RSQRT throughput probe had self-loop serialization: acc=rsqrt(acc) serializes each chain on itself even with 8 'independent' chains. Throughput unmeasured — needs redesign (separate output accumulator).
D1: simd_broadcast (lane0→all) dep-chain. ~13.9 cycles. Remarkably: = L1 global load latency (10.07ns ~13cyc). All cross-lane ops cost the same.
D2: simd_shuffle_down(delta=1) dep-chain. ~13.2 cycles. = L1 load tier. All simd permutation ops converge at ~13 cycles.
D2b: simd_shuffle_xor(mask=1) dep-chain. ~13.3 cycles. Adjacent-lane butterfly step. All three D1/D2/D2b within 0.5ns of each other and of L1 load.

B4 fix: 16 independent air.fast_rsqrt.f32 chains confirmed in AIR dump. WORSE than 8-chain (9.39ns) and dep-chain (9.41ns) — AGX instruction window saturates at ~8 long-latency ops. fast_rsqrt is non-pipelineable: T_eff = L = 12 cycles.

B5 fix: 16 independent fdiv arcp chains confirmed in AIR. WORSE than 8-chain (6.27ns). AGX instruction window for recip: optimal at 8 chains. 16-chain T_eff=10.8cyc vs 8-chain T_eff=8.1cyc — diminishing returns beyond window size.

metric: 30 dispatches x 100K inner x 4 SGMM calls
notes: D4: simdgroup_multiply_accumulate f32 8x8 dep-chain. 24.235 ns/call = ~31.5 GPU cycles. Each call = 512 MADs (1024 FP ops). Effective 0.024 ns/FP-op = 71× scalar fadd. Faster than simd_sum (36.8 cyc)! 32 threads share one matrix computation.

D3: simd_prefix_inclusive_sum dep-chain. 23.720 ns/call = ~30.8 GPU cycles. 2.32× single shuffle (10.2 ns). log2(32)=5 butterfly stages complete in 2.15× naive serial (5×13=65 cyc); hardware pipelining saves ~52% vs naive. FASTER than simd_sum (36.8 cyc)! prefix avoids the final broadcast step.

D3b: simd_prefix_exclusive_sum dep-chain. 22.275 ns/call = ~29.0 GPU cycles. 1.73 ns cheaper than inclusive — no final self-element add needed. Confirms inclusive scan adds one extra step (identity element write back). 1.45× single shuffle.

metric: 30 dispatches x 100K inner x 4 SGMM calls (4 independent acc)
notes: D4b: SGMM f32 8x8 throughput probe — 4 independent C accumulators. 24.384 ns/call = ~31.7 cycles. ILP gain vs dep-chain: 0.994× — EFFECTIVELY ZERO. Conclusion: simdgroup_multiply_accumulate is NON-PIPELINEABLE on M1 AGX. T_issue = T_latency = ~32 cycles. Hardware matrix unit does not accept new operands before previous result is consumed.

F5: MTLSharedEvent CPU wakeup latency — time from GPU encodeSignalEvent to CPU exit from waitUntilSignaledValue. min=5000 ns, p50=10000 ns, mean=10830 ns, p95=17000 ns, p99=30000 ns, max=44000 ns. Confirms C overhead decomposition: C=3,153,956 ns = 10,000 ns MTLSharedEvent + ~3,143,956 ns GPU command buffer / 1024-thread kernel launch infrastructure.

B4: metal::precise::rsqrt emits air.rsqrt.f32 = Newton-Raphson multi-step firmware (NOT hardware fast_rsqrt). ~31 cycles latency — 2.6x slower than air.fast_rsqrt.f32 (12 cyc). NOT a single HW instruction. Always use rsqrt() (fast) unless IEEE correctness required.

B5: recip fdiv arcp with 16 independent iter-derived inputs. 9.5 ns = 12.4 cyc — slightly above dep-chain (11 cyc / 8.6 ns). Iter-derived overhead dominates over any TP gain. Best recip TP = 8.1 cyc (8-chain dep-feedback, original probe_recip_tp).
B4 final: 2-chain air.fast_rsqrt.f32 dep-feedback gives 31.9 cycles (WORSE than dep-chain 12.2 cyc!) — precise::rsqrt dep-chain, not fast. Confirms precise::rsqrt is multi-step: latency much longer.
B4: 4-chain precise::rsqrt, 29.6 cycles. All multi-chain probes with precise::rsqrt confirm ~30 cyc latency. M1 fast_rsqrt (air.fast_rsqrt.f32) dep-chain = 12 cyc, non-pipelineable (T=L=12).

B5: metal::fast::divide(1.0f, acc) dep-feedback FOLDED by compiler — fast_div(1, fast_div(1, x))=x identity detected. Near-zero elapsed (151800 ns < C). Cannot use fast::divide for dep-chain TP measurement.

metric: single thread x 100K inner x 8 loads
notes: C3: L1 tier pointer-chase (4KB buffer, step=20B < cache-line). 64 cycles — 5x slower than sequential L1 (T6: 13 cyc). Reflects AGX wait-instruction overhead for serialized dependent load chains vs pipelined sequential loads.

metric: single thread x 100K inner x 8 loads
notes: C3: L2 tier (16KB > L1=8KB). 92 cycles — clear jump from L1 tier (64 cyc). Entering L2 per-core.

metric: single thread x 100K inner x 8 loads
notes: C3: L2/L3 boundary (64KB ≈ 768KB L2 total / 8 cores = 96KB/core). 109 cycles. Values plateau from 64KB to 512KB — all in same tier (L3/system = 8MB).

metric: single thread x 100K inner x 8 loads
notes: C3: L3 tier (256KB > 96KB L2/core). 110 cycles. Plateau: L1=64 cyc, L2=92-109 cyc, L3=110+ cyc (all buffers 64KB-512KB in same tier).

F6: PyTorch MPS dispatch overhead — identity FC 64-dim. p50=195µs min=160µs p95=249µs p99=284µs. MPS lower bound ~160-195µs for any model invocation. Dispatch overhead dominates for matrices < ~256x256.
F6: PyTorch CPU dispatch — identity FC 64-dim. p50=5µs min=5µs p95=5µs. Pure CPU inference: ~5µs for tiny model.
F6: MLMultiArray(64) alloc+fill overhead (CoreML API, no actual model dispatch). p50=46.6µs — CoreML framework base overhead before any model computation.

B6: IMUL64 dep-chain via probe_int64_mul_lat. LLVM folds 32 serial `mul i64 acc, 1664525` to 1 per iter (constant 1664525^32). ns/op=(4006860-3153956)/100000=8.529ns=11.1cyc@1.3GHz. Ratio to int32 mul (3.376ns): 2.53x. Lower than P's IMUL(64x64=64)=16cyc — compiler uses hardware IMUL(32x32=64)=8cyc path for multiply-by-constant.
